name: Darjeeling Himalayan Railway
description: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, also known as the DHR or the Toy Train, is a 2 ft gauge railway that runs between New Jalpaiguri and Darjeeling in the Indian state of West Bengal.

detailedInfo.introduction: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, also known as the 'Toy Train', is a 2 ft narrow-gauge railway that reflects the ingenuity of 19th-century pioneers.
detailedInfo.history: Developed by Franklin Prestage to transport tea more efficiently and provide a comfortable journey to the hill station of Darjeeling.
detailedInfo.architecture: Masterpiece of mountain engineering, using unique loops and reverses. The coal-fired steam engines are heritage gems.
detailedInfo.significance: A global icon of mountain travel and the soul of Darjeeling's tourism industry, featured in many films.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Tickets for the 'Joy Ride' should be booked via IRCTC. Best time: October to May.

name: Kaziranga National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, Kaziranga National Park is famous for the world's largest population of one-horned rhinoceroses.

detailedInfo.introduction: Kaziranga National Park is located in the floodplains of the Brahmaputra River in Assam, globally renowned for the endangered one-horned rhinoceros.
keyFacts: Location: Golaghat and Nagaon districts, Assam
Notable fauna: One-horned rhino, Tiger, Elephant
detailedInfo.history: Originally protected in 1905 due to efforts of Mary Curzon, it has evolved into one of the world's most successful conservation stories.
detailedInfo.architecture: Defined by tall elephant grass, marshland, and tropical forests, rejuvenated annually by monsoon flooding.
detailedInfo.significance: Global benchmark for wildlife protection and home to many endangered species.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Open from November to April. Jeep or elephant safaris are available across four main ranges.

name: Jagannath Temple Puri
description: The Jagannath Temple is an important Hindu temple dedicated to Jagannath, a form of Vishnu, in Puri in the state of Odisha on the eastern coast of India.

detailedInfo.introduction: The Jagannath Temple of Puri is a sacred Hindu temple and one of the four most holy places of pilgrimage in India (Char Dhams).
keyFacts: History: Rebuilt from the 10th century onwards
Highlights: Rath Yatra festival, world's largest kitchen
detailedInfo.history: The temple was built by King Anatavarman Chodaganga Deva. It is famous for its massive annual Rath Yatra (Chariot Festival).
detailedInfo.architecture: The temple is built on an elevated platform in the center of Puri. The main temple is a curvilinear structure crowned with the 'srichakra' of Vishnu.
detailedInfo.significance: It is a major spiritual center where millions gather for the Rath Yatra. The temple's kitchen (Mahaprasad) is the largest in the world.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Open to Hindus only. Non-Hindus can view the temple from a nearby platform. Best time: June/July during Rath Yatra.

name: Nalanda Ruins
description: Nalanda was a renowned mahavihara, a large Buddhist monastery, in the ancient kingdom of Magadha in India. The site is located about 95 kilometres southeast of Patna near the city of Bihar Sharif.

detailedInfo.introduction: Nalanda was a renowned Mahavihara, a large and revered Buddhist monastery in the ancient kingdom of Magadha in India. It is one of the world's first residential universities.
keyFacts: Highlights: Temple No. 3, ancient dormitories, Nalanda Archaeological Museum
detailedInfo.history: Flourished under the patronage of the Gupta Empire and later Harshavardhana. It attracted scholars from China, Korea, Japan, Tibet, and Turkey. It was eventually destroyed by Bakhtiyar Khalji in 1193.
detailedInfo.architecture: The ruins reveal highly organized layout with central courtyards, classrooms, and small dormitories for monks. The bricks depict the sophisticated engineering of the time.
detailedInfo.significance: Nalanda is a symbol of India's intellectual heritage and its leading role in global education during the classical age. It remains a place of profound scholarly pilgrimage.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Best time: October to March. The site is about 90 km from Patna and 12 km from Bihar Sharif. The museum across the road is a must-visit.

name: Kamakhya Temple
description: The Kamakhya Temple is a Shakta temple dedicated to the mother goddess Kamakhya. It is one of the oldest of the 51 Shakti Pithas.

detailedInfo.introduction: The Kamakhya Temple is a Shakta temple dedicated to the mother goddess Kamakhya. It is situated on the Nilachal Hill in western part of Guwahati city in Assam.
keyFacts: Significance: One of the 51 Shakti Pithas
detailedInfo.history: The temple has ancient origins and has been rebuilt several times, notably by the Koch king Naranarayan in the 16th century. It is the center of Tantric worship in India.
detailedInfo.architecture: The temple has a unique 'Nilachal' style of architecture, with a beehive-like dome of brick or stone. The inner sanctum is a natural cave with a spring.
detailedInfo.significance: It is a vital pilgrimage site for devotees of Shaktism. The Ambubachi Mela, celebrating the annual menstruation of the goddess, is its most important festival.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Best time: October to April. Expect long queues during festival seasons. Reach via taxi or shared rickshaws from Guwahati city center.
